authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for remote merchandise order handling. This guidance defines delay notices, cancellation handling, prompt refunds, and order-record expectations that materially affect installation scheduling and rebooking when delivery commitments move.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: delivery dependency intake, delay handling, and cancellation or refund boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC administers Magnuson-Moss warranty rules and explains seller, warrantor, and service-contract boundaries that matter when install failures, replacement requests, or post-install issues could drift into warranty adjudication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: installation exception routing, post-install issue classification, and warranty-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: California Civil Code Section 1723
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California Legislature publishes the controlling statutory text on posted refund policies and fallback refund or exchange obligations for covered retail sales, making it an authoritative jurisdiction-specific constraint on cancellation and refund escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance. The page explains when unresolved merchant refund or charge issues should move from store coordination into payment-dispute handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order, payment, refund mismatch handling and next-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: CPSC Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. safety regulator for many consumer electronics and appliances. The handbook defines stop-sale, segregation, recall communication, and reverse-logistics planning expectations relevant when scheduled installation inventory is recalled or unsafe.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall stop conditions, quarantine, and customer-impact safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Reverse Logistics Brochure
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA is the primary federal authority for hazardous-material transportation. Its reverse-logistics guidance is directly applicable when damaged, battery-containing, or recalled units discovered during installation require controlled return routing rather than routine handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: damaged product, battery, and hazardous transport stop-condition handling

workflow_stages.name: Coordination intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, channel, order, booking, product, installer, and site-readiness context before any routing or closure output is produced.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Booking, order, payment, and policy dependency mapping
workflow_stages.description: Build the dependency chain across POS, OMS, CRM, booking, inventory, and service systems to determine whether installation can proceed, be blocked, or be rerouted without drifting into pricing or refund authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team handoff and ownership resolution
workflow_stages.description: Identify prior owner, next owner, destination acceptance, appointment conflicts, duplicated ownership, and required artifact completeness for safe delivery-to-install or store-to-field-service routing.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception, refund, and policy-aware escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move delayed-install, cancellation, refund, promotion, financing, and promised-service conflicts into explicit escalation states instead of silently forcing an answer the lane does not own.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, and damaged-product stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve recall, damaged-unit, transport, and fraud-sensitive boundaries whenever an installation request carries customer-impact, loss-prevention, or hazardous-handling implications.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Issue closure only when destination owner, blocker resolution, and required evidence are explicit and any stricter tenant or jurisdiction rules have been surfaced rather than guessed.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: sales orders, invoices, delivery records, and installation bookings
customer account notes, reschedule requests, cancellation requests, and service tickets
POS transactions, OMS states, CRM records, booking calendars, and dispatch records
SKU, model, serial, promotion, financing, tender, refund, and credit records
inventory availability, backorder state, install kit completeness, and site-readiness confirmations
recall notices, damage reports, hazardous handling notes, and fraud or asset-protection case references

allowed: Classify installation coordination requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Track dependencies, duplicated ownership, and blocker state across retail and field-service systems.
Produce handoff summaries, dependency trackers, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Required order, booking, payment, site-readiness, installer, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund approval, legal interpretation, warranty adjudication, fraud decisioning, licensed trade judgement, or safety signoff.
A public baseline and tenant, jurisdiction, financing, manufacturer, or partner policy materially conflict.
A recall, damaged unit, billing dispute, or fraud signal changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Book or complete installation work without destination owner, required artifacts, or blocker resolution.
Override pricing, refund, promotion, safety, or fraud policies without explicit authority.
Authorize use, shipment, or installation of recalled, damaged, quarantined, or hazardous products.

domain_failure_modes: installation is handed off without site-readiness proof, required kits, or destination-owner acceptance
order and payment conflicts are collapsed into a generic scheduling answer and the wrong team executes the next step
the lane waives cancellation, refund, or promotion policy instead of routing to the correct approver
duplicate ownership between store, delivery, and installer teams causes the customer appointment to be dropped
a recalled or damaged appliance is routed toward installation instead of quarantine and safety escalation
fraud, chargeback, or promised-service disputes are treated as ordinary service friction and handled without the correct owner
installation coordination drifts into direct technical install approval or generic customer-service behavior

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific installer scope, local permit or licensed-trade rules, utility hookup rules, and OEM installation restrictions may materially differ by product family and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, CFPB, California legislative, CPSC, and PHMSA sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, tenant service model, partner network, and product-family constraints before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, customer-impact handling, and failure behavior instead of only generic delivery scheduling.

The lane stops at coordination. Pricing exceptions, refunds, warranty adjudication, fraud findings, technical installation decisions, and safety signoff remain outside the lane even when customer pressure is high.
